human_verification.notes: Reviewed occupant experience outputs for boundary discipline, comfort and service communication quality, work-order evidence handling, occupancy and inspection gating, and construction-specific escalation behavior.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to occupant-facing facilities service handling, evidence-backed communication, and escalation packaging rather than technical signoff or generic customer support.
Authoritative sources cover occupant complaint handling, safety-controlled maintenance boundaries, work-order evidence, facilities service delivery, and occupancy gating with refresh discipline.
Research scenario families confirm permit or site-readiness gaps, inspection or closeout blockers, licensed-trade or code-boundary handling, and the queue-required service-support exception cases.
Evaluation scenarios cover missing detail, repeat failure, unsupported policy exception requests, cross-lane handoff, occupancy blockers, low-confidence escalation, and terminology normalization.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for a future retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across policy-exception, repeat-failure, permit, inspection, safety, and licensed-trade escalation cases.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
